Name: curvature_aware_adaptive_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Adaptive Routing for Efficient Neural Architectures
Short Hypothesis: Utilizing curvature-aware adaptive routing mechanisms can optimize neural network architectures for better performance and computational efficiency, reducing overhead while preserving the expressiveness of learned representations.
Related Work: Existing literature has explored geometry-adaptive methods in Riemannian manifolds, notably in meta-learning contexts (Gao, 2024). However, this proposal specifically focuses on integrating curvature-aware routing strategies into established neural architectures, addressing optimization inefficiencies in real-time inference scenarios, which have not been sufficiently tackled in prior work.
Abstract: This proposal aims to investigate the implementation of curvature-aware adaptive routing mechanisms within neural network architectures to enhance performance while minimizing computational overhead. By employing a training-free representation geometry framework, we will explore how curvature diagnostics can inform adaptive routing decisions in real-time inference tasks. Our approach will leverage spectral quantization and deterministic optimization proxies to enforce mathematical transparency and numerical stability. The proposed methodology will be validated through a series of experiments comparing traditional fixed-architecture networks against our curvature-aware adaptive designs, measuring performance metrics such as accuracy, inference time, and resource utilization. By establishing a theoretical foundation for curvature-aware routing, this research will contribute to the development of efficient neural architectures that maintain expressiveness while addressing practical deployment constraints.
Experiments: 1. Compare performance metrics (accuracy, inference time, resource utilization) of a standard neural architecture against a curvature-aware adapted version on benchmark datasets.
2. Implement curvature diagnostics to inform adaptive routing decisions during inference, analyzing the impact on gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning.
3. Evaluate the robustness of the curvature-aware adaptive network against varying levels of data noise and scarcity, measuring generalization capabilities.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include overfitting to specific datasets if the curvature diagnostics are not generalized. Additionally, the complexity of implementing adaptive routing may introduce unforeseen computational overheads, which need to be monitored and controlled. Limitations may also arise from the need for extensive empirical validation to ensure theoretical claims translate into practical improvements.
